@round On your second example gif, you might also benefit from using a grid with your force layout so things snap better like https://t.co/d2qXzwv38J or https://t.co/ViOlnSuGpB

@round Depends a lot on how your data is shaped - could be more precise with a data example - most likely you would use https://t.co/p8jrdWNE3p - or if you are summarizing things d3.rollup - but yeah, you need to transform your data in JSON that is made of nodes and links to start
@round https://t.co/oCDrc4Y5b0

@round I think you basically want a combination of force layout (for nodes and links) and then you would use forceX and forceY to push things to their position in the hierarchy, like https://t.co/gmXmPEMOdL or almost https://t.co/mufwcI0eEy
